item.title: The Artistry of Tarot
item.description: Also: the modern reggae of Original Koffee, Tina Fey’s modern take on “The Four Seasons,” Hugh Jackman’s gory Robin Hood, and more.

item.title: Chronicle of a Disaster Foretold
item.description: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan’s “Regime Change” is packed with news about the Trump White House that will stay news.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Remnick

item.title: Herding the Fro-Yo Sheep
item.description: This summer, every trendy dessert joint has a mile-long line of transplants and tourists. One New Yorker is protesting in his own way—by “baa”-ing at them.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Kamp

item.title: The Natural Memory of Kabir Sehgal
item.description: For his new album, “Stars and Static 2026,” the fourteen-time Grammy winner recorded sounds from across the country. Did it change how he thinks about America?
item.dc:creator: ByDan Greene

item.title: Bruce Nauman Isn’t Bound by the Rules
item.description: At a gallery in Tribeca, the artist talked bald spots with Eric Fischl and walked through his quickie exhibition “No Mistakes,” 3-D videos of him drawing with his eyes closed.
item.dc:creator: ByThessaly La Force

item.title: Postscript: Mark Singer
item.description: In a 1997 Profile for the magazine, he looked for Donald Trump’s soul. Where it should have been he found—nothing.
item.dc:creator: ByIan Frazier

item.title: The Billionaires’ Vagina Club
item.description: With her motto, “Sexual health is health,” Dr. Sally Greenwald aims to optimize orgasms for the women of Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: ByMelanie Thernstrom

item.title: Are Humanoid Robots Ready to Be Deployed?
item.description: Neo and a dozen other robots with human forms are scheduled to hit the market. Experts are nervous.
item.dc:creator: ByStephen Witt

item.title: Did a Climber Leave His Girlfriend to Die at the Top of a Mountain?
item.description: An Austrian court pieces together the mysterious circumstances of a couple’s disastrous hike.
item.dc:creator: ByWilliam Finnegan

item.title: The Tick That Hunts Down Its Hosts—Including Us
item.description: Lone-star ticks don’t just pursue and bite people. The affliction they’re spreading, an allergy to red meat known as alpha-gal syndrome, attacks a way of life.
item.dc:creator: ByBurkhard Bilger

item.title: OnlyFans Creators Bare All
item.description: Up close with sex workers of the streaming era.
item.dc:creator: ByJennifer WilsonPhotography byKaty Grannan

item.title: My Great Meeting with God
item.description: He was very complimentary. People tell me, “Mr. President, you put the fear of God in God.”
item.dc:creator: ByPatricia Marx

item.title: “Pig Lab”
item.description: In this upside-down world there’d be a pig like Ted Waters, who, one blue winter night outside Marjah, had his leg blown off by a bomb disguised as a guardrail.
item.dc:creator: ByWill Mackin

item.title: What Happened to Your Face?
item.description: How the human countenance became something to study, edit, optimize, and scan.
item.dc:creator: ByCal Revely-Calder

item.title: Something Is Very Wrong with Modern Longevity Science
item.description: A new book argues that many of the world’s oldest people aren’t so old after all.
item.dc:creator: ByDhruv Khullar

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “The Infinity Machine,” “If This Be Magic,” “While We Were Waiting,” and “Coyoteland.”

item.title: The Fibre Fad Keeps On Moving
item.description: How a nutritional trend brought bathroom talk into the realm of food culture.
item.dc:creator: ByHannah Goldfield

item.title: The Met’s “Costume Art” Makes a Case for Fashion
item.description: From its new galleries off the museum’s Great Hall, the Costume Institute seeks to put clothing at the center of art history.
item.dc:creator: ByRachel Syme

item.title: Sublime Fury at the Ojai Festival
item.description: In an idyllic setting, Leila Josefowicz and Esa-Pekka Salonen delivered an explosive performance of Ligeti’s Violin Concerto.
item.dc:creator: ByAlex Ross

item.title: At Pacha New York, an Infamous Night Club Is Reborn
item.description: After the Brooklyn Mirage—a popular but troubled music venue—was torn down, a glitzy Ibiza institution took its place.
item.dc:creator: ByKelefa Sanneh

item.title: “Campbell’s Tomato Soup”
item.description: “Begin with the flavor, which is not rank but insipid.”
item.dc:creator: ByCampbell McGrath

item.title: “requiem for a barber”
item.description: “on monday all must rest, so monday it will stay.”
item.dc:creator: ByJan Wagner

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the July 6 & 13, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Wednesday, June 24, 2026
item.description: Today’s theme: Body positivity.
item.dc:creator: ByRebecca Goldstein
